sse.description: Server-Sent Events fallback for proxies or environments that strip the WebSocket Upgrade header. Server-to-client only: identical envelope shape, no client subscription control frames or WHEP signaling.
sse.title: SSE realtime channel (fallback)

Sec-WebSocket-Protocol.description: Subprotocol selection; makes the envelope major explicit.

query.description: Optional authentication token as a query parameter.

ws.description: Bidirectional WebSocket channel. The server sends event envelopes; the client sends subscription control frames ($subscribe, $unsubscribe, $set_rate, $resume, $pong). Negotiated subprotocol: `multiview.v1` (or `multiview.bin.v1` for compact binary meter frames).
ws.title: WebSocket realtime channel

AlarmTransition.name: AlarmTransition
AlarmTransition.summary: An alarm was raised, updated, cleared, or acknowledged (topic `alarms`).
AlarmTransition.title: Monitoring alarm lifecycle event

Alert.description: The `key` field is a stable dedupe identifier for the condition; multiple `alert.raised` frames with the same key coalesce rather than stacking.
Alert.name: Alert
Alert.summary: An alert was raised (alert.raised) or cleared (alert.cleared) on topic `alerts`.
Alert.title: Operator alert raised or cleared

AudioMeter.description: Emitted on topic `audio.meters`. This is the sole high-rate conflated topic; frames are conflated/sampled at 10–30 Hz on the wire. A slow consumer can only lose its own meter frames — the engine never back-pressures. The binary fast-path (subprotocol `multiview.bin.v1`) carries the same fields in compact form; this schema describes the decoded (JSON) shape.
AudioMeter.name: AudioMeter
AudioMeter.summary: Per-input/track peak/RMS/clip meter sample (numeric only, never audio data).
AudioMeter.title: High-rate audio meter sample

Envelope.description: Every message in both directions uses this envelope. The `t` field is the discriminator selecting the `data` schema. The `seq` field is a per-connection monotonic cursor; gaps indicate dropped frames and trigger resume/resync. The `ts` field carries engine-monotonic nanoseconds (same clock family as output PTS). `v` is the envelope schema major; clients MUST reject unknown majors.
Envelope.name: Envelope

corr.description: Optional correlation id echoing a REST command / job.

data.description: Typed payload selected by `t`. Schema depends on the event type.

seq.description: Per-connection monotonic resume cursor. A gap means frames were dropped.

t.description: Dotted event type; the discriminator selecting the `data` schema.

topic.description: Subscription routing key. Control frames use `$control`.

ts.description: Engine monotonic nanoseconds (same clock family as output PTS).

v.description: Envelope schema major. A receiver MUST reject an unknown major.

Envelope.summary: The single wire frame used for all realtime messages (ADR-RT002).
Envelope.title: Versioned realtime envelope

HealthWarning.description: A richer sibling of `Alert` carrying a stable `code`, the affected `subsystem`, and a concrete `remediation`. The `code` is the dedupe key: frames with the same code coalesce rather than stacking. Capability-mismatch codes (e.g. `gpu-present-no-vulkan-adapter`) are latched build-time facts — raised once, cleared on reconfigure/restart.
HealthWarning.name: HealthWarning
HealthWarning.summary: A health warning was raised (health.warning.raised) or cleared (health.warning.cleared) on topic `alerts`.
HealthWarning.title: Actionable health warning raised or cleared

Hello.name: Hello
Hello.summary: First server frame after auth; advertises connection parameters.
Hello.title: $hello control frame

InputConnection.name: InputConnection
InputConnection.summary: An input source transitioned connection state (input.connection on topic `inputs`).
InputConnection.title: Input source connection state change

InputStreams.description: Read-only discovery: every elementary stream (video / audio tracks / subtitles / SCTE-35 / KLV / timecode) the input offers, each with a stable kind-scoped id. Emitted on first appearance and as a delta on re-probe / PMT-version bump.
InputStreams.name: InputStreams
InputStreams.summary: An input's stream inventory appeared or changed on re-probe (input.streams on topic `inputs`).
InputStreams.title: Input elementary-stream inventory

JobProgress.name: JobProgress
JobProgress.summary: Progress of a REST command job correlated by `corr` (topic `jobs`).
JobProgress.title: Long-running job progress

OutputStatus.description: Emitted on topic `outputs`. Make-before-break migration emits `migrating` during the parallel-output window.
OutputStatus.name: OutputStatus
OutputStatus.summary: An output sink changed run state (starting/running/migrating/error).
OutputStatus.title: Output sink status update

SalvoEvent.name: SalvoEvent
SalvoEvent.summary: A salvo was armed, taken, or cancelled (topic `tally`).
SalvoEvent.title: Salvo arm/take lifecycle

Subscribe.name: Subscribe
Subscribe.summary: Client requests subscription to one or more topics.
Subscribe.title: $subscribe control frame (client to server)

SystemMetrics.description: Emitted on topic `system`. A high-rate conflated lane like `audio.meters`: samples are latest-only at ~1-2 Hz on the wire and excluded from the lossless replay ring. PUSHED, never polled — a slow UI loses only its own samples and the engine never back-pressures (inv #10). Live values only; historic windows (the data decisions are made from) are a separate cold REST query.
SystemMetrics.name: SystemMetrics
SystemMetrics.summary: CPU / GPU / encoder-decoder utilisation sample (numeric only).
SystemMetrics.title: High-rate whole-system metrics sample

TallyEvent.name: TallyEvent
TallyEvent.summary: Resolved tally state for one tile/element changed (topic `tally`).
TallyEvent.title: Tally lamp/UMD state change

TileState.description: Emitted on topic `tiles` whenever a tile's state machine transitions. Invariant #2: Live → Stale → Reconnecting → NoSignal. The compositor holds the last-good frame while STALE/RECONNECTING; renders a placeholder on NO_SIGNAL.
TileState.name: TileState
TileState.summary: A tile transitioned between lifecycle states (LIVE/STALE/RECONNECTING/NO_SIGNAL).
TileState.title: Tile state machine transition

TilesSnapshot.description: Emitted once at connect (after `$hello`) on topic `tiles` with `t` `$snapshot` (realtime.md §5): the snapshot-then-delta baseline (`snapshot ⊕ ordered deltas = current truth`, ADR-RT003). A receiver MUST treat it as a state REBUILD, never a merge. Despite the `$`-prefixed tag it rides its data topic, not `$control`.
TilesSnapshot.name: TilesSnapshot
TilesSnapshot.summary: Full current per-tile lifecycle state a fresh client rebuilds its tile cache from.
TilesSnapshot.title: Connect-time tiles baseline ($snapshot on topic tiles)

AlarmTransition.description: Data body of `alarm.raised`, `alarm.updated`, `alarm.cleared`, `alarm.acked`. Carries the current AlarmRecord value after the transition (X.733-aligned).

record.description: The current alarm record after the transition.

Alert.description: Data body of `alert.raised` and `alert.cleared` events. The `key` field is a stable dedupe identifier: multiple raised frames with the same key coalesce.

active.description: Whether the condition is currently active.

key.description: Stable dedupe key for the condition.

AudioMeter.description: Data body of the `audio.meter` event: a high-rate per-input/track peak/RMS/clip sample. This is numeric metadata only — never audio content. Conflated/sampled at 10–30 Hz on the wire (high-rate lane).

clip.description: Whether any channel clipped in this window.

overflow.description: Whether the meter pipeline overflowed (dropped windows).

GpuMetrics.description: A per-GPU utilisation sample. Optional fields are absent where the vendor does not expose that signal.

encoder_sessions.description: DEVICE-WIDE active concurrent encode sessions (NVIDIA) across all processes.

id.description: Stable device identity (UUID where available, else an index).

self_compute_util.description: Our process's compute (SM) utilisation on this GPU, 0.0-1.0.

self_encoder_sessions.description: Encode sessions owned by our process on this GPU.

self_mem_used_bytes.description: VRAM (bytes) attributed to our process on this GPU.

GpuVendor.description: GPU/accelerator hardware vendor (selects which per-engine signals to expect).

HealthWarning.description: Data body of `health.warning.raised` and `health.warning.cleared`. A richer sibling of `Alert`: the `code` is the stable dedupe key, and `remediation` carries the concrete fix. Capability-mismatch codes are latched build-time facts (raised once, cleared on reconfigure/restart).

active.description: Whether the condition is currently active (raise vs clear).

message.description: A clear, human-readable description of the condition.

remediation.description: The concrete remediation — what the operator must do to fix it.

since.description: When the condition was first raised (engine monotonic nanoseconds).

Hello.description: Data body of `$hello`: the first server frame after auth.

default_rate_hz.description: Default wire cadence when `rate_hz` is omitted from `$subscribe`.

server_v.description: Envelope schema majors this server can speak.

InputStreams.description: Data body of the `input.streams` event: an input's full elementary-stream inventory (RT-3).

input_id.description: The owning input's configured source id.

inventory.description: The StreamInventory (multiview_core::stream::StreamInventory): every elementary stream the input offers.

input_id.description: The input id the inventory is bound to, if known.

streams.description: Every elementary stream, in container order.

JobProgress.description: Data body of `job.progress`; correlated to a REST command via the envelope `corr`.

Lag.description: Data body of `$lag`: this connection's queue overflowed for a topic.

LagAction.description: What the server did with the dropped frames.

LifecycleState.description: Tile/input lifecycle state (invariant #2: Live → Stale → Reconnecting → NoSignal).

OutputRunState.description: Running state of an output sink.

clients.description: Number of currently-connected consumers, if known.

ProtocolError.description: Data body of `$error`: a control-plane error.

Resume.description: Data body of `$resume` (client→server): present last-seen cursor on reconnect.

Resync.description: Data body of `$resync`: the gap is unrecoverable; the client MUST rebuild state from the fresh snapshot that follows.

head.description: Output head this recall applies to, if scoped.

phase.description: The lifecycle phase this event reports.

SetRate.description: Data body of `$set_rate` (client→server): change a topic's wire cadence.

rate_hz.description: Requested cadence (Hz); server clamps to [min, max].

Subscribe.description: Data body of `$subscribe` (client→server): subscribe to topics.

rate_hz.description: Optional max cadence (Hz); server clamps and reports effective.

since_seq.description: Optional resume cursor: subscribe + replay from after this seq.

Subscribed.description: Data body of `$subscribed`: per-topic ack before the snapshot.

snapshot_seq.description: The `seq` the forthcoming snapshot is current as of.

SystemMetrics.description: Data body of the `system.metrics` event: a high-rate whole-system sample (cpu / gpu / encoder-decoder). Numeric only; conflated at ~1-2 Hz (high-rate lane).

gpus.description: Per-GPU utilisation samples; empty on a GPU-free host.

mem_used_bytes.description: Host memory in use (bytes), if known.

program_fps.description: Aggregate program output rate (fps), if running.

TallyEvent.description: Data body of `tally.state`: resolved tally lamp/UMD state for one element.

TallyTarget.description: What a tally state applies to (tagged by `kind`).

TileSnapshotEntry.description: One tile's current lifecycle state inside a `tiles` `$snapshot`. The `id` is the same key the sparse `tile.state` deltas scope their envelope `id` with, so a snapshot-rebuilt cache and its delta patches address the same rows.

id.description: The tile id (the bound source id in the run projection).

input.description: The input bound to the tile, if known.

TileState.description: Data body of the `tile.state` event: a tile lifecycle transition.

input.description: The input bound to the tile at the time of the transition, if any.

TilesSnapshot.description: Data body of the `tiles`-topic `$snapshot` frame: the full current per-tile lifecycle baseline sent once at connect (after `$hello`). A receiver MUST rebuild (replace) its tile state from it, never merge.

as_of_seq.description: The engine state sequence this baseline is current as of.

Unsubscribe.description: Data body of `$unsubscribe` (client→server): stop receiving topics.

WarningCode.description: Stable catalog code of a health warning (kebab-case). `#[non_exhaustive]`: the catalog grows over time, so a client must treat an unknown code as a forward-compatible warning, not an error.

WarningSeverity.description: Severity of a health warning (sibling of AlertSeverity).

info.description: WebSocket and SSE realtime event stream for the Multiview engine. Every message uses a single versioned envelope (ADR-RT002). Topics: tiles, inputs, outputs, audio.meters, alerts, alarms, tally, layout, config, logs, jobs, preview, system, capabilities. The WebSocket endpoint (/api/v1/ws) is bidirectional (events + subscription control frames); the SSE endpoint (/api/v1/events) is server-to-client only.
info.title: Multiview Realtime API

subscribe-sse.description: Receive all event types on the SSE fallback channel.
subscribe-sse.title: Receive realtime events (SSE)

subscribe-ws.description: Receive all event types on the WebSocket channel after subscribing to the desired topics via $subscribe control frames.
subscribe-ws.title: Receive realtime events (WebSocket)

sse-server.description: Server-Sent Events endpoint (server→client only). Fallback transport.

host.description: Hostname and optional port of the Multiview daemon. IPv6-first: the default is the IPv6 loopback `[::1]`; override with your host.
Hostname and optional port of the Multiview daemon. IPv6-first: the default is the IPv6 loopback `[::1]`; override with your host.
